steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains that traveling waves break — and are height-constrained — once steepness exceeds a critical threshold. The key finding is that spike waves can exceed that constraint. The question asks what the passage most strongly supports about spike waves.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: oversimplifies the finding; the passage's point is the violation of the expected limit, not a blanket claim about size. ✗
  • Choice B: implies steepness still limits spike waves, which is the opposite of the passage's point. ✗
  • Choice C — spike waves may be higher than expected based on traveling wave properties: directly captures that spike waves exceed the height constraint that governs traveling waves. ✓
  • Choice D: shifts focus to traveling waves rather than the exceptional behavior of spike waves. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
